input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — `AppThemeBinding` markup extension

Replace every hardcoded color with an `AppThemeBinding` that supplies a `Light` and `Dark` value:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=Black}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, every bound property updates automatically — no event handlers needed.

### C# equivalent — `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme`

For colors there's a convenience method:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing
BackgroundColor = Colors.White; // fallback
this.SetAppThemeColor(
    BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
```

For non-color properties, use the generic version:

```csharp
var img = new Image();
img.SetAppTheme<string>(
    Image.SourceProperty,
    "logo_light.png",  // light
    "logo_dark.png");   // dark
```

### Key points

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Responds to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` internally |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, strings, images, etc.) |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force a specific theme |
| **Resources** | You can also theme entire `ResourceDictionary` styles by wrapping values in `AppThemeBinding` inside a `Style` |

This approach requires no platform-specific code and works on iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS.
